topic: Agentic AI mitigates LLM hallucinations in academic literature reviews.
final_review:
The integration of Agentic AI in academic literature reviews has the potential to mitigate Large Language Model (LLM) hallucinations. According to (Dwivedi et al., 2023), generative conversational AI, such as ChatGPT, can be used to generate text, but it also raises concerns about the accuracy and reliability of the generated content. (Sallam, 2023) highlights the promising perspectives and valid concerns of using ChatGPT in healthcare education, research, and practice, which can be applied to academic literature reviews as well.

The use of Agentic AI can help to address the issue of LLM hallucinations by providing a more transparent and explainable approach to text generation. (Barredo Arrieta et al., 2019) discuss the concept of Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) and its potential to provide insights into the decision-making process of AI models. This can be particularly useful in academic literature reviews, where the accuracy and reliability of the generated content are crucial. Furthermore, (Page et al., 2021) provide guidance on reporting systematic reviews, which can be applied to the use of Agentic AI in academic literature reviews.

By following a systematic approach, researchers can ensure that the generated content is accurate, reliable, and transparent. (Clark, 2013) discusses the concept of predictive brains and situated agents, which can inform the development of Agentic AI models. The potential of Agentic AI to mitigate LLM hallucinations in academic literature reviews is also highlighted by (Dwivedi et al., 2023) and (Kasneci et al., 2023), who discuss the opportunities and challenges of using large language models in education and research.

The application of Agentic AI in academic literature reviews can provide a more transparent and explainable approach to text generation, addressing concerns about accuracy and reliability. (Barredo Arrieta et al., 2019) and (Page et al., 2021) provide a foundation for the development of Agentic AI models that can mitigate LLM hallucinations. Overall, the integration of Agentic AI in academic literature reviews has the potential to improve the accuracy and reliability of generated content, and (Dwivedi et al., 2023), (Sallam, 2023), and (Kasneci et al., 2023) demonstrate the importance of considering the role of Agentic AI in this context.

References:
Barredo Arrieta, A., Díaz-Rodríguez, N., & Del Ser, J. (2019)
Clark, A. (2013)
Dwivedi, Y. K., Kshetri, N., & Hughes, L. (2023)
Kasneci, E., Seßler, K., & Küchemann, S. (2023)
Page, M. J., Moher, D., & Bossuyt, P. M. (2021)
Sallam, M. (2023)
